author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text:
Gurus are really quacks. I will just say Osho was hilarious an entertaining. He actually ended up with 82 or 83 Rolls Royces. I am not sure you know he was a very popular guy in USA and took over a town in Oregon in the late 1970s and early 80s, with abou 500 000 hippies flocking to his presence and living there on a ranch. His wife Sheila poisoned local shops' fresh farm produce to prevent people from going to the local elections so they could take over the municipality. However Osho didn't like his wife who was manipulating him and the entire community (Osho is very mesmerising, mind you, please do yourself a favour for the sake of amusement, he really does not try to control things like others his talks are interesting and off the cuff, sometimes scripted. But it's remarkable what he come up with when asked a philosophical question from some new age hippy that really blindsides him. How he manages to connect the dots is amazingly amusing). There is some suspicion whether either his wife Sheila or the CIA (who banned him from the USA as he was becoming too influential and people were becoming fanatical in really large numbers, he was truly charismatic, with a glittering hat and a fancy Rolex, he's really an entertainer, can't always follow what he says as he's just trying to be funny or is ridiculously outdated).. - he died of thallium poisoning and I don't beleive it was ever solved as to whether it was the CIA or Sheila, this was during the Regan-era administration when I was born ...I'm sometimes overexcited if I hear something interesting you say . I know very little about science but there are some things I have learned here, like still wanting to try a levitating magnetic rotor generator for AC induction. Again I shall be using styrafoam as I can't afford to melt m own tetrateanite into the clay molds I would specifically want, as one can't 3D print tetrateanite, lol...! If only I can get some tetrateanite and mold them (tetrateanite magnets) in clay then I can create exactly what I want in regards the shape of my desired magnets.... There's not much else I'm interested in making. But, you would love looking at 1) Osho an amusing guru, 2) Jaron Lanier  (the Prime Unifying Scientist of Microsoft who works in the Office of the Chief Technology Officer which in total comes to OCTOPUS. Jaron was working with Marvin Minski and they had some fundamental argument which I don't recall now. Anyways, just for interest's sake, that is why I was talking about asking whether the universe could be a computer, as now IBM is about to soon make a revelation about their quantum computer, which uses close to zero absolute temperatures. If the Universe has these temperatures in abundant locations, or as a sub-planar phase of frequential reality (Star Trek talk), then informaton would be rapidly transmitted if not instantaneously. Ursula LeGuin talked about such a thing called an "ansible" in her short but highly entertaining book, "The Left Hand of Darkness" which I had the odd luck of reading. Coincidentally now there is a Linux server deployment tool called "Ansible" which has nothing to do with her ansible. Kind of lke a cosmic telephone. I'm just amusing myself so apologise if I offend. And I quote, "The term was actually introduced by science fiction writer and physicist Ursula K. Le Guin in her 1966 novel "Rocannon's World." An ansible is a fictional device that enables instantaneous communication across vast interstellar distances, allowing for faster-than-light communication between different parts of the universe. The concept of the ansible has since become a popular trope in science fiction literature." So I'm thinking if at such temperatures of superconductivty, quantum computation may be possible and the universe is just calculating away at horrendous rates. But it's just my lay perspective on quantum mechanics. Jaron is also very charismatic and a pleasure to listen to, he actually invented virtual reality many many years ago and is no fool. HE was speaking about the possibility of the universe being a "computer" possibly in the Lex Friedman Podcast. Lex is a PHD in AI... who interviews tons of people.



)

author: @musikSkool
text: I have found that when I am researching a subject I need to know buzzwords that I can google to find more information.  "Technical jargon" really speeds up my research because if I know what the experts call something then I can find expert opinions on something.  Like if I knew the fancy word for race-ready suspension geometry that is far better than road suspension then I could google that and find people explaining why it works, or maybe get pictures of it with arrows pointing to the forces involved.  I like to call this a magic word because once I know it, its like magic, and I can then find all sorts of useful information.

author: @petercordingley927
text: I love this channel, great advise without being pretentious. I have just recently finish a book called 'Steal like an artist' by Austin Kleon an absolutely amazing book which falls into your mindset. There nothing truly original anymore (Egyptians complained about this 4000 years ago haha)  most people have copied what others have done and put their own spin or mark on it, Either improving it or taking it in a different direction. I have just started reading his other book about sharing your ideas and processes, it really reflects your work and I highly recommend his books.

author: @rickdworsky6457
text: Speculation isn't terrible, but insistence upon the verifibly impossible is worthless. The universe consisrently follows its own patterns... or we couldn't do science. Neither blind faith nor temper tantrums bend rules or alter reality to our liking. Evolution happened, and Mars isn't flat either.
